questions.text: The Chemistry of carbon compounds is known as
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Biochemistry is the branch of science that explores the chemical processes within and relating to living organisms. While it involves many carbon compounds, it specifically focuses on biological molecules like proteins, DNA, and lipids rather than all carbon-based substances.

Inorganic chemistry generally deals with compounds that do not contain carbon-hydrogen bonds. This includes minerals, metals, and organometallic compounds, though it does cover a few simple carbon compounds like carbonates and oxides of carbon.

This is the correct choice. Organic chemistry is scientifically defined as the study of the structure, properties, composition, reactions, and preparation of carbon-containing compounds. This includes not only hydrocarbons but also compounds with any number of other elements.
This option is incorrect because Option C accurately identifies the branch of chemistry dedicated to the study of carbon compounds.

questions.explanation: carbon is unique because it can form long chains and rings, a property called catenation. Because carbon forms millions of different compounds, scientists created a specific branch called organic chemistry to study them. Originally, it was thought these chemicals only came from living things, but we now know they can be synthesized in labs as well.

questions.text: First organic compound synthesized in the laboratory was
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Citric acid is a common natural compound found in citrus fruits and not the first organic compound synthesized in the laboratory.
Acetic acid is a simple carboxylic acid but not the first organic compound synthesized in the laboratory.
Urea was the first organic compound synthesized in the laboratory by Friedrich Wöhler in 1828, marking a significant milestone in organic chemistry.
Glucose is a common sugar and not the first organic compound synthesized in the laboratory.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: Urea. Urea holds the distinction of being the first organic compound synthesized in the laboratory, a groundbreaking achievement in the history of organic chemistry. Citric acid, acetic acid, and glucose are all important organic compounds, but were not the first to be synthesized.

questions.text: Organic compounds are studied separately from inorganic compounds because
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Organic molecules are essentially made up of carbon atoms joined covalently with one another
options.explanation: Organic compounds are unique because of catenation, where carbon atoms link together in long chains or rings through strong covalent bonds. This complex structural variety is not typically seen in the inorganic world, necessitating a dedicated field of study.

options.text: Organic compounds are generally insoluble in water but soluble in organic solvents
options.explanation: Most organic compounds are non-polar and do not dissolve well in polar water, whereas many inorganic salts do. Their preference for non-polar solvents (like benzene or ether) is a distinct physical characteristic that sets them apart from typical inorganic substances.

options.text: Organic reactions are molecular in nature and are slow and complicated
options.explanation: Unlike inorganic reactions, which often involve fast ion exchanges, organic reactions involve the breaking and forming of stable covalent bonds. This makes their reactions slower, more complex, and often requiring specific conditions like catalysts or high heat.

This is the correct choice because all the individual points (A, B, and C) highlight fundamental differences in bonding, solubility, and reactivity. Together, these unique traits justify why organic chemistry is treated as a separate branch from inorganic chemistry.

questions.explanation: Organic compounds behave very differently from inorganic ones. While inorganic substances usually involve ions and quick reactions, organic molecules are built like complex puzzles using covalent bonds. Because they have unique ways of melting, dissolving, and reacting, scientists found it much easier and more organized to study them as a separate group.

questions.text: Petroleum is refined by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This technique is used to separate substances that are heat-sensitive and do not mix with water, like essential oils. It works by passing steam through the mixture to lower the boiling points of the components so they don't decompose.

This method is used for liquids with very high boiling points that might break down if heated to their normal boiling temperature. By reducing the pressure (creating a vacuum), the liquid boils at a much lower temperature, which is helpful for heavy oil fractions.

This is the standard process for refining petroleum because crude oil is a complex mixture of hydrocarbons with different boiling points. A fractionating column allows these components to be separated into groups (fractions) like petrol, diesel, and kerosene based on their specific boiling ranges.

Activated charcoal is primarily used for adsorption to remove impurities, odors, or colors from a liquid or gas. While it is great for purifying water or decolorizing sugar, it cannot separate the various hydrocarbon parts found in crude oil.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is fractional distillation because petroleum (crude oil) contains many different hydrocarbons with varying sizes and boiling points. In a refinery, the oil is heated and sent into a tall tower; the lighter parts, with lower boiling points, rise to the top, while the heavier parts stay at the bottom. This allows us to collect useful products like gasoline and jet fuel separately. Other methods like steam or vacuum distillation, are too specific for certain chemicals, and charcoal is only for cleaning/filtering rather than separating a whole mixture.

questions.text: Which of the following is a good source of organic compounds
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Coke is the solid, black residue left behind after the destructive distillation of coal. It is almost pure carbon (about 98%) and is primarily used as a fuel or as a reducing agent in the extraction of metals like iron, rather than as a source for various organic molecules.

Coal tar is a thick, black liquid produced during the processing of coal. It is a very rich source of organic compounds, containing a mixture of more than 200 different substances, including many aromatic compounds like benzene, phenol, and naphthalene, which are essential for making dyes and drugs.

While coal is the starting "raw material" that contains organic matter from ancient plants, it is a complex solid mineral. We must process it (through heating) to actually extract the useful organic liquids and gases; therefore, coal itself is considered the parent source rather than the direct source of specific organic chemicals.

This option is incorrect because coal tar is globally recognized in chemistry as a primary industrial source for a wide variety of aromatic organic compounds used in everything from perfumes to explosives.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is coal tar because it is a complex mixture that contains hundreds of different organic substances. When coal is heated strongly in the absence of air (a process called destructive distillation), it breaks down into coke, coal gas, and coal tar. While coke is just leftover carbon, the coal tar liquid is full of valuable aromatic molecules like benzene and toluene. These chemicals are then separated from the tar using fractional distillation to be used as starting materials in the chemical industry.

questions.text: The thermal decomposition of organic compounds in the absence of air is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Combustion is a chemical reaction where a substance reacts rapidly with oxygen, releasing heat and light. Unlike the process described in the question, combustion requires the presence of air or oxygen to burn the organic material completely into carbon dioxide and water.

Pyrolysis is the chemical decomposition of organic materials at high temperatures in the total absence of oxygen. The word comes from the Greek words "pyro" (fire) and "lysis" (separating), meaning the heat is used to break down large molecules into smaller ones without burning them.

Polymerization is a process where small molecules, called monomers, combine chemically to produce a very large chainlike or network molecule called a polymer. This is essentially the opposite of decomposition, as it involves building up larger structures rather than breaking them down.

Synthesis is a general term in chemistry for the execution of chemical reactions to form a complex product from simpler starting materials. It focuses on creating new compounds through bond formation, whereas the question asks about the breakdown of compounds through heat.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is pyrolysis because it specifically refers to breaking chemical bonds using heat (thermal) while preventing the material from catching fire by removing air. In FSc organic chemistry, this is often called "cracking" when applied to alkanes. While combustion needs oxygen to release energy, pyrolysis uses external heat to "crack" large organic molecules into smaller, useful gases, liquids, and solids like charcoal.

questions.text: In petroleum industry the thermal decomposition of alkanes or paraffins is known as
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pyrolysis is the general scientific term for the chemical decomposition of any organic material using heat in the absence of oxygen. While cracking is technically a type of pyrolysis, the term pyrolysis is broader and can apply to wood, plastics, or waste, rather than being the specific industrial term used for petroleum fractions.

Cracking is the specific industrial term used in the petroleum industry for breaking down long-chain, heavy hydrocarbon molecules into smaller, more useful ones like gasoline and ethene. It involves high temperatures and often catalysts to "crack" the carbon-to-carbon bonds in alkanes to meet the high demand for lighter fuels.

Combustion refers to the process of burning a fuel in the presence of oxygen to produce heat and carbon dioxide. While combustion is a common chemical process, it is not the specific process described in the question.
This option is incorrect because cracking is a well-established and essential chemical process in oil refineries worldwide to increase the yield of high-value products from crude oil.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is cracking because it is the industry-specific application of thermal decomposition for alkanes. Since crude oil provides more heavy oils than the market needs, refineries use cracking to break these large molecules into smaller ones like petrol. While pyrolysis is the general name for heating without air, cracking is the term you will see in your FSc/A-Level chemistry books when discussing the production of gasoline and alkenes. This process helps convert surplus heavy fractions into more valuable, high-demand fuels.

questions.text: What are the important sources of organic compounds
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Plants are a massive primary source of organic compounds. Through the process of photosynthesis, they produce complex molecules like carbohydrates (cellulose, starch, sugars), vitamins, and oils. Many medicinal compounds, such as quinine or morphine, are also extracted directly from plant tissues.

Animals provide a variety of essential organic substances that are necessary for life and industry. Key examples include proteins (like those found in meat and silk), fats, enzymes, and various hormones. These compounds are often more complex and specifically structured compared to those from mineral sources.

These are known as fossil fuels and are the largest industrial sources of organic compounds. Petroleum provides thousands of hydrocarbons, natural gas is a primary source of methane, and coal tar contains a rich mixture of aromatic compounds like benzene and naphthalene used to make dyes and drugs.

This is the correct choice because organic chemistry originally started with the study of compounds from living things (plants and animals). Over time, we discovered that fossil fuels (which are actually ancient buried plant and animal matter) and laboratory synthesis are equally vital sources.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is all of the above because organic compounds are found everywhere in nature and industry. Plants and animals are living sources that provide us with biological molecules like proteins and sugars. On the other hand, coal, petroleum, and natural gas serve as mineral sources that provide the raw materials for fuels, plastics, and synthetic chemicals. Together, these sources supply the vast majority of the carbon-based molecules we use in our daily lives, from the food we eat to the gasoline in our cars.

questions.text: The process of distillation is employed for the purification of liquids from
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Volatile impurities are substances that evaporate easily at lower temperatures. If a liquid has volatile impurities, they will vaporize along with the liquid being purified and condense together in the receiving flask. Therefore, simple distillation is not an effective way to remove them unless their boiling points are significantly different.

Non-volatile impurities are substances like salts, minerals, or solid contaminants that do not turn into vapor when heated. During distillation, the liquid turns into gas and leaves these impurities behind in the heating flask. This makes distillation an excellent method for separating a pure liquid from solid or non-boiling waste.

Insoluble impurities are solids that do not dissolve in the liquid, such as sand or dust. While distillation can separate a liquid from them, it is not the primary method used. Usually, simpler and faster techniques like filtration or decantation are employed to remove insoluble solids before any distillation begins.

This option is incorrect because distillation has limitations. It cannot easily separate a liquid from "all" impurities, especially if those impurities have boiling points very close to the liquid (volatile impurities) or if they form azeotropes (mixtures that boil at a constant temperature).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is non-volatile impurities because the principle of distillation relies on turning a liquid into a vapor and then back into a liquid. Since non-volatile substances (like salt in water) cannot turn into vapor at the boiling point of the liquid, they stay behind in the original flask while the pure liquid travels through the condenser. While filtration is used for insoluble dirt, distillation is specifically chosen when we need to get rid of dissolved solids or heavy components that simply won't evaporate.

questions.text: Which element is the essential constituent of organic compounds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sulfur is found in certain organic molecules, especially in specific amino acids like cysteine and in various proteins. While it is essential for life processes and helps in the formation of strong "disulfide" bridges in hair and nails, it is not present in every organic compound.

Hydrogen is found in almost all organic compounds, particularly hydrocarbons and their derivatives. Because it is so common, many people think it is the primary element. However, in the hierarchy of organic chemistry, it is considered a partner to the central atom rather than the defining essential constituent.

Nitrogen is a key part of organic compounds like proteins, DNA, and RNA. It is very important for the biological functions of living things, but many entire groups of organic compounds, such as carbohydrates, fats, and simple alkanes, do not contain any nitrogen atoms at all.

Carbon is the "backbone" of all organic chemistry. By definition, organic chemistry is the study of carbon-containing compounds. Carbon is unique because it can form four strong covalent bonds and link together in long chains or rings, providing the framework for every single organic molecule in existence.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is carbon because the very definition of an organic compound is a substance that contains carbon atoms covalently bonded to other elements. Carbon is essential because its ability to form four bonds allows it to build complex and diverse structures like the chains in fuels or the rings in medicines. While hydrogen, nitrogen, and sulfur are often present, they are attached to the carbon skeleton. Without carbon, the field of organic chemistry would not exist, as every organic molecule, from simple methane to complex human DNA, must contain at least one carbon atom.

questions.text: What is the chemical formula of prussian blue
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the number of Iron atoms is not in accordance with the chemical formula of prussian blue.
This is the correct chemical formula for prussian blue, as it correctly represents the composition of the compound.
This option is incorrect as it does not match the correct chemical formula of prussian blue.
This option is incorrect as one of the provided options is indeed the correct chemical formula for prussian blue.

questions.explanation: The correct chemical formula of prussian blue is Fe4[Fe(CN)6]3. This compound consists of iron (III) cations and hexacyanoferrate (II) anions, resulting in a 4:3 ratio of iron to cyanide complex. Option A has an incorrect ratio of iron atoms, Option C has an incorrect number of iron atoms, and Option D is incorrect as one of the provided options is correct.

questions.text: Most of the hydrocarbons from petroleum are obtained by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cracking is a chemical process used after the initial separation. It breaks down larger, heavy hydrocarbon molecules into smaller, high-demand ones like gasoline. While it is essential for increasing the supply of specific fuels, it is not the primary way we obtain the original natural mixture of hydrocarbons from crude oil.

Pyrolysis is the general term for thermal decomposition in the absence of air. Although cracking is a form of pyrolysis, this term is rarely used in the petroleum industry to describe the bulk separation of hydrocarbons. It is more commonly associated with converting biomass or waste into fuels.

This is the fundamental process used in every oil refinery to separate the hundreds of hydrocarbons found in petroleum. It relies on the fact that different hydrocarbons have different boiling points. By heating crude oil, the various components rise through a tower and are collected as separate "fractions" based on their size and boiling range.

Destructive distillation is the process of heating a solid material, like coal or wood, in a closed vessel without air to break it down into various liquid and gaseous products. This is the method used for coal, but for liquid petroleum, we use fractional distillation to separate the existing liquids.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is fractional distillation because petroleum is naturally a mixture of many different hydrocarbon liquids already present together. The refinery's first job is to separate this mixture into groups like petrol, diesel, and kerosene without changing their chemical structure. This is done in a fractionating column where the oil is heated; the lighter hydrocarbons with lower boiling points rise to the top, while the heavier ones stay lower down. Other processes like cracking are secondary steps used to chemically "resize" these fractions to meet market needs.

questions.text: When petroleum is heated granually at 20-60°C the batch of vapours evolved will be rich in
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Petroleum ether is a very light fraction consisting of short-chain alkanes (mostly pentane and hexane). Because these small molecules have very weak intermolecular forces, they evaporate at low temperatures, typically between 20°C and 60°C, making them the first to be collected.

Kerosene is a medium-weight fraction containing hydrocarbons with about 10 to 16 carbon atoms. These molecules are larger and heavier, so they require much more heat to boil. In a refinery, kerosene is usually collected in a much higher temperature range, often between 175°C and 275°C.

Lubricating oil consists of long-chain hydrocarbons that are quite heavy and viscous. These compounds have high boiling points, often exceeding 350°C. They do not vaporize at the low temperatures of 20-60°C and are usually separated under vacuum to prevent them from breaking down.

Paraffin wax is a solid at room temperature and is made of very large hydrocarbon chains. It has an extremely high boiling point and is usually obtained from the residue left over after the lighter and medium fractions have already been distilled off at much higher temperatures.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is petroleum ether because the boiling point of a hydrocarbon depends on the size of its molecules. At a low temperature range of 20-60°C, only the lightest molecules with the shortest carbon chains have enough energy to turn into vapor. Petroleum ether is the lightest liquid fraction, so it comes off first. Heavier products like kerosene, lubricating oils, and waxes stay in the liquid or solid state until the temperature is raised much higher in the distillation tower.

questions.text: Organic compounds are very large in number because of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon has four valence electrons, so it can form four covalent bonds with other atoms (H, O, N, halogens, etc.). This allows carbon to form a very large variety of stable compounds with different structures.
Carbon has a strong ability to bond with itself, forming long chains, branched chains, and rings (C–C–C–C…). This property greatly increases the number and complexity of organic compounds.

options.text: Ability of C to form multiple bond
options.explanation: Carbon can form single (C–C), double (C=C), and triple (C≡C) bonds. This leads to different types of compounds such as alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and many functional groups, further increasing the diversity of organic compounds.

Organic compounds are extremely large in number because carbon possesses all three properties: tetravalency, catenation, and the ability to form multiple bonds. Together, these factors allow carbon to form countless stable and varied compounds.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is all of the above because carbon is like a "universal building block" in nature. Its tetravalency allows it to branch out in four directions, while catenation lets it build long backbones of atoms. When you add its ability to form double and triple bonds, the number of possible combinations becomes nearly infinite. Other elements might have one of these properties, but only carbon combines all of them so effectively, which is why there are more carbon compounds than those of all other elements put together.

questions.text: Coal tar is obtained as a by product during destructive distillation of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Coal is the primary material used in the destructive distillation process to produce coal tar. Therefore, this is the correct answer.
While wood can undergo destructive distillation to produce products like charcoal, it does not produce coal tar.
Bones are used in processes like bone char production, not in coal tar production through destructive distillation.
Coke is a product obtained from coal and used as a fuel; it does not produce coal tar during destructive distillation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Coal. Coal tar is obtained as a byproduct during the destructive distillation of coal. This process involves heating coal in the absence of air, resulting in various byproducts, including coal tar. The other options are incorrect because wood, bones, and coke do not undergo destructive distillation to produce coal tar. Wood can be destructively distilled to produce charcoal, bones are not relevant to this process, and coke is a result of coal processing but does not yield coal tar.

questions.text: The linking of two or more molecules of a substance to form a large single molecule is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fusion typically refers to the merging of two or more things to form a single entity, but in organic chemistry, the process described involves the formation of a larger single molecule from multiple smaller molecules.

Synthesis involves the creation of a compound from simpler materials, which can be a correct term in chemistry, but in this context, we are specifically looking for the term that describes the linking of multiple molecules to form one large molecule.
Correct! Polymerization is the process of combining multiple molecules to form a larger single molecule, typically involving the repetition of a specific unit.

Polymorphism refers to the ability of a material to exist in different forms or crystal structures, which is not directly related to the process of linking multiple molecules to form one large molecule.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Polymerization.' In organic chemistry, polymerization refers to the process of linking two or more molecules of a substance to form a large single molecule, known as a polymer. This process often involves repeating units to create the polymer chain. Fusion, Synthesis, and Polymorphism are incorrect as they do not specifically describe the process of combining multiple molecules to form one large molecule, as polymerization does.

questions.text: All such organic compounds having open chain structure of carbon are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Aromatic compounds are a special class of cyclic organic molecules that contain at least one benzene ring or a similar stable ring structure. Unlike open-chain compounds, these molecules are closed loops and have unique stability and odors, which is why they are called "aromatic."

Aliphatic compounds are organic molecules where carbon atoms are joined together in straight chains, branched chains, or non-aromatic rings. The term is most commonly used to describe "open-chain" hydrocarbons like alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes, which do not contain the specialized benzene-style bonding.

Alicyclic compounds are "aliphatic cyclic" compounds. This means they have carbon atoms joined in a ring (cyclic), but they behave chemically like open-chain aliphatic compounds rather than aromatic ones. Examples include cyclobutane or cyclohexane, which are closed loops, not open chains.
This option is incorrect because the term "aliphatic" is the standard scientific classification used to describe all open-chain organic compounds (also known as acyclic compounds).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is aliphatic because this category covers all organic compounds with an open-chain structure. These chains can be perfectly straight or have branches coming off the sides, but the ends of the carbon chain never meet to form a ring. While aromatic and alicyclic compounds focus on closed-ring shapes, aliphatic compounds include the simplest hydrocarbons like methane and propane. The name comes from the Greek word for "fat," as many early open-chain compounds were derived from animal fats and oils.

questions.text: Closed chain compounds in which ring is made up of carbon atom along with some other atom are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Homocyclic compounds are those in which the ring structure is made up of only one kind of atom. In organic chemistry, this almost always refers to rings made exclusively of carbon atoms. These do not contain any other elements like nitrogen or oxygen within the actual circle of the ring.

Carbocyclic is simply another name for homocyclic compounds, where the ring consists entirely of carbon atoms. Common examples include benzene or cyclohexane. Since the question mentions "other atoms" being present in the ring, this option is incorrect as it excludes anything except carbon.

The prefix "hetero-" means different. Heterocyclic compounds are cyclic structures that contain at least one atom of an element other than carbon (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or sulfur) as part of the ring. These "different" atoms are specifically known as heteroatoms.

Alicyclic compounds are rings that behave like open-chain (aliphatic) compounds. While they are closed chains, they can be either homocyclic or heterocyclic. This term refers more to the chemical behavior and "fat-like" nature of the compound rather than the specific types of atoms making up the ring.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is heterocyclic because it describes a ring that is not "pure" carbon. In these molecules, a heteroatom like oxygen or nitrogen takes the place of a carbon atom in the ring's cycle. For example, in a molecule like pyridine, one carbon in a six-membered ring is replaced by nitrogen. This is different from carbocyclic or homocyclic rings where the entire loop is made only of carbon. Understanding this distinction is very important in FSc because heterocyclic compounds form the basis of many biological molecules like DNA bases and vitamins.
hints: ring made up of carbon atom

questions.text: All closed chain compounds processing a hexagonal ring of six carbon atoms are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Aliphatic compounds do not have the specific hexagonal ring structure described in the question.
Correct! Compounds with a hexagonal ring of six carbon atoms are classified as aromatic compounds in organic chemistry.
Carbocyclic compounds may have different ring structures, not necessarily a hexagonal ring of six carbon atoms.
This option is incorrect as the correct answer is an aromatic compound with a hexagonal ring of six carbon atoms.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Aromatic compound' because compounds with a hexagonal ring of six carbon atoms are specifically referred to as aromatic compounds in organic chemistry. Aliphatic compounds do not have this specific ring structure, while carbocyclic compounds may have different ring sizes. 'None of the above' is incorrect as the given compounds with the hexagonal ring structure fall under the category of aromatic compounds.

questions.text: Pentane (C5H12) has how many isomers
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect because alkanes starting from butane can have their carbon atoms arranged in more than one way. Since pentane has five carbon atoms, there are multiple unique ways to connect these atoms while still following the rules of valency.

This is incorrect because it misses one of the possible arrangements. While you can form a straight chain and a chain with one branch, there is also a third possible structure where the carbon atoms are even more highly branched around a central point.

This is the correct answer. Pentane (C5H12) can exist in three distinct structural forms: the straight-chain "n-pentane," the single-branched "isopentane" (2-methylbutane), and the double-branched "neopentane" (2,2-dimethylpropane).

This is incorrect and is a common mistake made by students who confuse the number of carbon atoms (five) with the number of possible isomers. For an alkane to have five or more isomers, it would need to have at least six carbon atoms (hexane has five).
This option incorrectly suggests that pentane has four structural isomers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is three isomers because there are exactly three ways to connect five carbon atoms and twelve hydrogen atoms using only single bonds. The first is a straight line (n-pentane). The second is a four-carbon chain with one methyl group branching off (isopentane). The third is a three-carbon chain with two methyl groups attached to the middle carbon (neopentane). Even though they all have the same formula, their different shapes give them different physical properties like boiling points.

questions.text: The empirical formula of a compound is CH2O. Its molecular mass is 180. The molecular formula of the compound is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This would be the result if the multiplier n was 3, but since the molecular mass is 180, this compound (mass 90) does not match the requirement.
This is the correct option because multiplying the empirical formula CH2O by 6 gives this result, and its total mass equals 180.
This represents a pentose sugar with a mass of 150, which is lower than the given molecular mass of 180.
This is the formula for acetic acid with a mass of 60, which only requires a multiplier of 2, not 6.

questions.explanation: To find the molecular formula from an empirical formula, we first calculate the empirical formula mass and then find the multiplier "n" by dividing the molecular mass by the empirical mass.The correct answer is B. The empirical formula mass of CH2O is 30 (12 for C, 2 for H, and 16 for O). By dividing the molecular mass (180) by the empirical mass (30), we get the whole number n = 6. Multiplying each atom in the empirical formula by 6 gives C6H12O6. The other options are incorrect because their calculated molecular masses do not equal 180.

questions.text: The existence of two or more chemical compounds with same molecular formulae but different structural formulae is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Structural isomerism refers to the existence of two or more chemical compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulae, making this the correct answer.
Optical isomerism is related to the arrangement of atoms in space around an asymmetric carbon atom, not the structural differences in compounds.
Cis-trans isomerism refers to the spatial arrangement around a double bond, not the overall structural differences in compounds.
This option is incorrect as the phenomenon described in the question specifically relates to structural isomerism.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Structural isomerism' because it precisely describes the situation where different chemical compounds have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements. Optical isomerism and cis-trans isomerism are specific types of isomerism that focus on different aspects of molecular structure, making them incorrect in this context. The option 'None of the above' is also incorrect as the question clearly pertains to the concept of structural isomerism.

questions.text: Ethanol and methoxy methane (dimethyl ether) can be represented by the same molecular formula
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the condensed structural formula specifically for ethanol, showing the hydroxyl group, but it does not represent methoxy methane.
This is the correct molecular formula for both compounds, as both contain exactly two carbon atoms, six hydrogen atoms, and one oxygen atom.
This is the formula for ethane, an alkane that does not contain oxygen and is unrelated to ethanol or ethers.
This formula represents compounds with three carbons, such as propanol or ethyl methyl ether, which have higher masses than ethanol.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B. Both ethanol and methoxy methane have the molecular formula C2H6O because they are isomers. In ethanol, the oxygen is part of an alcohol group (-OH), while in methoxy methane, the oxygen is sandwiched between two methyl groups (C-O-C). Because they have the same molecular formula but different functional groups, they are called functional group isomers.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as homologues do not differ by 12 in molecular masses.
This is the correct answer as homologues typically differ by 14 in molecular masses due to the addition of a CH2 group.
This option is incorrect as homologues do not differ by 16 in molecular masses.
This option is incorrect as homologues do not differ by 18 in molecular masses.

questions.explanation: Homologues in organic chemistry are compounds with the same functional group but differing by a repeating CH2 unit, leading to a 14 difference in molecular masses between consecutive homologues. Therefore, the correct answer is Option B: 14. Options A, C, and D are incorrect as homologues do not differ by 12, 16, or 18 in molecular masses, respectively.

questions.text: The general formula of alkanes is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is correct because it accurately represents the general formula for alkanes, indicating that for every 'n' carbon atoms, there are '2n+2' hydrogen atoms, which reflects the saturation of these hydrocarbons.
This option is incorrect as it represents the general formula for alkenes, which contain at least one double bond between carbon atoms, making them unsaturated.
This option is incorrect because it does not represent alkanes; instead, it suggests a formula that would apply to alkynes, which have triple bonds and are also unsaturated.
This option is incorrect as it implies a direct 1:1 ratio of carbon to hydrogen atoms, which is not applicable to alkanes. Alkanes must follow the '2n+2' rule for saturation.

questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with single bonds, and their general formula is CnH2n+2.

questions.text: A group of atoms in a compound that is responsible for the characteristic reactions is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option refers to a group that reacts easily but may not necessarily determine the characteristic reactions of a compound.
This option specifically relates to groups that are electron deficient and attract electrons during reactions, but it may not cover all characteristic reactions of a compound.
While nucleophilic groups are important in reactions, they do not encompass all characteristic reactions of a compound.
This is the correct answer. Functional groups are specific groups of atoms that determine the characteristic reactions of a compound.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Functional group.' In organic chemistry, a functional group is a group of atoms within a molecule that is responsible for the characteristic chemical reactions of that molecule. The other options, the reactive group, the electrophilic group, and the nucleophilic group, while important in organic chemistry, do not encompass the concept of functional groups and their role in determining characteristic reactions.

questions.text: Which of the following are devoid of any function group
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes are hydrocarbons that consist of only single bonds between carbon atoms, making them devoid of any functional groups.
Alkenes contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond, which is a functional group, so they are not devoid of functional groups.
Alkynes contain at least one carbon-carbon triple bond, which is a functional group, so they are not devoid of functional groups.
This option is incorrect as alkanes do not have any functional groups, making them devoid of any functional groups.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Alkanes. Alkanes are hydrocarbons that only contain single bonds between carbon atoms, hence they are devoid of any functional groups. Alkenes and alkynes both contain carbon-carbon multiple bonds, which are functional groups. Therefore, they are not devoid of functional groups. The option 'None of the above' is incorrect because alkanes do not have any functional groups, making them the only choice devoid of functional groups.

questions.text: -X is a functional group of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group (-OH), not X.
Alkyl groups consist of carbon and hydrogen atoms attached to a parent molecule, not X.
Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with single bonds between carbon atoms, not X.
Halides consist of a halogen atom (e.g., Cl, Br, F) bonded to a carbon atom, making it the functional group X in this case.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Halide. In organic chemistry, a halide functional group consists of a halogen atom bonded to a carbon atom. In this question, X refers to the halide functional group. Alcohols (Option A), alkyl groups (Option B), and alkanes (Option C) do not fit the description of X in this context.

questions.text: -OH is the functional group of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkyl halides contain –X (halogen: F, Cl, Br, I) as the functional group, not –OH.
Alcohols contain the hydroxyl group (–OH) attached to an alkyl (sp3 carbon) group.
Phenols also contain the –OH group, but it is attached directly to an aromatic (benzene) ring.
The –OH (hydroxyl) group is the functional group of both alcohols and phenols; the difference lies only like the carbon to which –OH is attached.

questions.explanation: The -OH group is called the hydroxyl group and it is the main functional group for both alcohols and phenols. In alcohols, this group is joined to an open chain of carbons (alkyl group), while in phenols, it is joined directly to a benzene ring. Because they both have the -OH group, they share some similar properties, like the ability to form hydrogen bonds.

questions.text: Olefinic double bond is the functional group of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes do not contain double bonds; they only have single bonds between carbon atoms.
Correct! Alkenes are compounds that contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond, making them olefinic compounds.
Alkynes contain carbon-carbon triple bonds, not double bonds.
This option is incorrect because alkenes specifically have the functional group of a double bond, so 'Alkenes' is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Alkenes. In organic chemistry, compounds with a double bond are referred to as olefinic compounds. Alkenes specifically contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond, distinguishing them from alkanes (single bonds) and alkynes (triple bonds). Therefore, the functional group of an olefinic double bond corresponds to alkenes, making them the correct choice. The other options, alkanes, alkynes, and 'None of the above,' do not fit the description of compounds with a double bond, thus making them incorrect choices.

questions.text: -C≡C- is the functional group of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes do not contain triple bonds (-C≡C-), they only have single bonds (-C-C-).
Alkenes contain double bonds (-C=C-) between carbon atoms, not triple bonds. This functional group is not present in alkenes.
Correct! Alkynes have triple bonds (-C≡C-) between carbon atoms, making this the functional group represented by -C≡C-.
This option is incorrect as the correct answer is Alkynes, which is one of the options provided.

questions.explanation: The functional group -C≡C- is characteristic of alkynes, which are hydrocarbons that contain a triple bond between carbon atoms. Alkanes have only single bonds, alkenes have double bonds, and the presence of a triple bond specifically indicates the presence of alkynes. Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: Alkynes. The other options are incorrect because they do not contain the triple bond characteristic of alkynes.

questions.text: Compounds that have the same composition but differ in structural formulae are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Polymers are large molecules (macromolecules) built by repeating many smaller units called monomers. They do not have the same composition as a single monomer; instead, they are much larger and have entirely different physical properties.

Isomers are compounds that share the same molecular formula (same composition) but have different arrangements of atoms. This means while they have the same "ingredients," the way those ingredients are "put together" differs.

A homologue refers to a member of a homologous series, where each member differs from the next by a constant unit, typically a -CH2- group. They have different molecular formulas and compositions, like Methane (CH4) and Ethane (C2H6).

This is simply an alternative spelling of "homologue." Just like option C, it refers to compounds in a series that differ by a repeating unit, so they do not have the same composition or molecular formula.

questions.explanation: Isomers are like two different buildings made from the same pile of bricks; the amount of material is the same, but the shape is different. Because their structures differ, isomers often have different physical properties like boiling points or densities. For example, n-butane and isobutane both have the formula C4H10, but one is a straight chain, and the other is branched.

questions.text: The first and simplest alkane is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ethane has two carbon atoms, making it more complex than the first alkane.
Correct! Methane is the first and simplest alkane with one carbon atom.
Propane has three carbon atoms, making it more complex than the first alkane.
This chemical formula represents ethyne or acetylene, not the first alkane.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Methane. In organic chemistry, the first and simplest alkane is methane, which consists of one carbon atom. Ethane, propane, and C2H2 have more carbon atoms in their structures, making them more complex and not the first alkane.

questions.text: Which of the following is a ketone
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is not a ketone. It contains an ether linkage (C-O-C), which lacks the carbonyl group (C=O) necessary for a ketone.
Option B is an aldehyde, not a ketone. It contains a carbonyl group, but it's bonded to a hydrogen atom, characteristic of aldehydes.
Option C is a ketone. It has a carbonyl group (C=O) bonded to two carbon atoms, which is the defining structure of a ketone.
Option D is a carboxylic acid, not a ketone. It has a carbonyl group bonded to a hydroxyl group (OH), which is characteristic of carboxylic acids.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C:C2H5-CO-C2H5 , which is a ketone. A ketone has a carbonyl group (C=O) bonded to two carbon atoms. Option A is an ether, which lacks a carbonyl group. Option B is an aldehyde because the carbonyl group is bonded to at least one hydrogen atom. Option D is a carboxylic acid, having a carbonyl group bonded to a hydroxyl group, not forming a ketone.

questions.text: C6H14 is the formula
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hexene is a compound with a double bond, but the formula given does not indicate the presence of a double bond. Therefore, this is not the correct answer.
Correct! Hexane is a straight-chain alkane with 6 carbon atoms, which matches the formula C6H14.
Hexyne is a compound with a triple bond, but the formula given does not indicate the presence of a triple bond. Therefore, this is not the correct answer.
This option is blank and does not provide any information. It is not a valid choice.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Hexane' because the formula C6H14 corresponds to a straight-chain alkane with 6 carbon atoms. Hexene and hexyne indicate the presence of double and triple bonds, respectively, which are not present in this formula. Benzene is not a valid choice as it does not match the molecular formula.

questions.text: Homologue differs from the proceeding member by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option does not indicate a structural difference; homologues differ by more than just one carbon atom
Correct! Homologues differ by CH2 groups in their structure, representing a difference of one methylene group
This option indicates a difference of one methyl group, which is not the defining difference between homologues
This option does not specify a structural difference and is a general statement rather than a specific difference between homologues

questions.explanation: Homologues are organic compounds that differ by a repeating unit of CH2 in their structure. This difference accounts for the change in molecular weight and physical properties as you move from one homologue to the next. Option B, CH2, correctly represents this structural difference. Options A, C, and D do not accurately reflect the defining difference between homologues, leading to their incorrectness.

questions.text: Alkynes have the general formula
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This formula represents alkanes, not alkynes. Alkanes have single bonds between carbon atoms.
This formula represents alkenes, not alkynes. Alkenes have a double bond between carbon atoms.
Correct! Alkynes have a triple bond between carbon atoms, resulting in the formula CnH2n-2.
This formula does not accurately represent the structure of alkynes. It is incorrect in the context of alkynes.

questions.explanation: The correct formula for alkynes is CnH2n-2, where n represents the number of carbon atoms in the alkyne chain. This formula accounts for the presence of a triple bond between carbon atoms, resulting in fewer hydrogen atoms compared to alkanes and alkenes. Option A represents alkanes, Option B represents alkenes, and Option D does not accurately depict the structure of alkynes, making them incorrect choices in this context.

questions.text: Which of the following is a heterocyclic compound
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benzene is a homocyclic compound consisting of only carbon atoms in its ring structure.
Pyridine is a heterocyclic compound with a ring structure containing both carbon and nitrogen atoms.
Toluene is a homocyclic compound similar to benzene with only carbon atoms in its ring structure.
Chlorobenzene is a homocyclic compound like benzene but with a chlorine substituent.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Pyridine. A heterocyclic compound is a compound with a ring structure containing at least one non-carbon atom, such as nitrogen, oxygen, or sulfur. Pyridine fits this definition as it has a ring structure composed of both carbon and nitrogen atoms. Benzene, Toluene, and Chlorobenzene are all homocyclic compounds as they contain only carbon atoms in their ring structures.

questions.text: Which of the following statement is correct about homologue
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The molecular mass of each homologue differs by 14
options.explanation: This statement is correct as the molecular mass difference between homologues is usually by a constant increment of 14 (CH2 group).

options.text: Homologues differs in their physical properties
options.explanation: This statement is generally true as homologues have similar chemical properties due to the same functional group but differ in physical properties.

options.text: Homologues have the same functional group in them
options.explanation: This statement is true as homologues are organic compounds with similar chemical properties due to the same functional group.

This statement is correct as homologues do have similar chemical properties due to the same functional group, they differ in physical properties, and the molecular mass difference between them is usually by a constant increment of 14 (CH2 group).

questions.explanation: Homologues are organic compounds that have similar chemical properties due to the same functional group but differ in physical properties. The statement 'All of the above' is correct because homologues indeed have the same functional group, differ in physical properties, and have a molecular mass difference usually by a constant increment of 14 (CH2 group). The other options are incorrect as they do not encompass all the characteristics of homologs.

questions.text: It's major constituents are hydrogen (50%) methane (35%) and carbon monoxide (8%)
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Coke is not a gas and is primarily composed of carbon.
Coal gas consists of hydrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide as mentioned in the question.
Coal tar is a byproduct of coal processing and is not primarily composed of the gases mentioned.
This option is incorrect as 'Coal gas' matches the composition described in the question.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Coal gas' because it is composed of hydrogen (50%), methane (35%), and carbon monoxide (8%) as stated in the question. 'Coke' is primarily carbon-based and not a gas. 'Coal tar' is a different byproduct of coal processing. 'None of these' is incorrect as 'Coal gas' matches the composition provided.

questions.text: Residue left after the fractional distillation of coal tar is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pitch is the thick, black, sticky residue that remains in the distillation flask after all the volatile oils (like benzene and naphthalene) have been boiled off. It is solid at room temperature and is widely used for roofing and surfacing roads.

This term is not related to the distillation of coal tar. In chemistry or metallurgy, "Dutch" might refer to Dutch metal (an alloy of copper and zinc), but it is not a residue or a byproduct of coal processing.
Gangue is a term used in metallurgy to describe the earthy or rocky impurities (like sand or clay) found mixed with a raw mineral ore. It has nothing to do with the liquid distillation of coal tar.

Matte is another metallurgical term referring to a crude mixture of molten metal sulfides produced during the smelting of sulfide ores, such as copper or nickel. It is not a product derived from coal.

questions.explanation: When coal tar is heated in a process called fractional distillation, different useful liquids are separated based on their boiling points. Once all these liquids (the "fractions") have evaporated and been collected, a heavy, dark material is left behind. This residue is called pitch. While the liquids are used to make dyes and drugs, the solid pitch is used as a binder for coal briquettes or for road construction.

questions.text: _is a mixture of methane, ethane, propane, and butane used as a fuel and for making other organic chemicals
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the lightest fraction collected at the very top of the distillation tower. It is a mixture of small gaseous hydrocarbons like methane, ethane, propane, and butane, which are used as fuel and as starting materials (feedstocks) for making other chemicals.

Also known as petrol, gasoline is a liquid mixture of slightly larger hydrocarbons, typically containing 5 to 10 carbon atoms (C5 to C10). While it is a major fuel for cars, it does not consist of methane or ethane, which are gases at room temperature.

Kerosene is a heavier liquid fraction containing hydrocarbons with about 11 to 13 carbon atoms (C11 to C13). It is primarily used as jet fuel and for domestic heating or lighting in lamps, rather than being a mixture of the simplest gaseous alkanes.

This fraction, often called diesel oil, consists of even larger molecules (C14 to C18). It is used as fuel for heavy vehicles like trucks and buses and for industrial heating, but it does not contain the light gases mentioned in the question.

questions.explanation: In a petroleum refinery, crude oil is separated into parts based on how heavy the molecules are. The very lightest part is the refinery gas, which contains the first four members of the alkane family: methane (CH4), ethane (C2H6), propane (C3H8), and butane (C4H10). These gases are either burned as fuel to run the refinery itself or processed further to create plastics, solvents, and other organic materials.

questions.text: On strong heating the fractions containing the larger hydrocarbon molecules are broken up into smaller and more volatile molecules this is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sublimation is the process of a substance transitioning directly from a solid to a gas without passing through the liquid phase. This does not involve breaking up hydrocarbon molecules.
Cracking is the process where larger hydrocarbon molecules are broken up into smaller and more volatile molecules upon strong heating. This is the correct answer.
Roasting typically refers to heating food, not the process of breaking up hydrocarbon molecules into smaller ones.
Refining involves purifying a substance or separating it into its components, but it does not specifically refer to breaking up hydrocarbon molecules into smaller ones.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Cracking.' When larger hydrocarbon molecules are subjected to strong heating, they undergo cracking, a process where they are broken up into smaller and more volatile molecules. This is a key concept in organic chemistry. The other options, sublimation, roasting, and refining, do not accurately describe the process of breaking up hydrocarbon molecules through strong heating.

questions.text: Cracking is also termed as
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pyrolysis is a Greek-derived word where "pyro" means fire and "lysis" means breaking. In chemistry, it refers to the thermal decomposition of organic materials at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen, which is exactly what happens during the cracking of alkanes.

Refining is the overall industrial process of purifying a crude substance, like petroleum, into useful products. While cracking is a part of the refining process used to increase gasoline yield, the two terms are not synonymous; refining also includes distillation and treatment.

Polymerization is the chemical process of joining many small molecules (monomers) together to form a very large chain-like molecule (polymer). This is the opposite of cracking, as cracking breaks large molecules down into smaller ones rather than building them up.

This is a specific type of chemical reaction where a hydrogen halide (like HCl or HBr) is added to an unsaturated hydrocarbon like an alkene. It is an addition reaction and has nothing to do with the thermal breakdown of large molecules into smaller fragments.

questions.explanation: Cracking and pyrolysis both describe the process of using high heat to break the strong carbon-to-carbon bonds in long-chain hydrocarbons, producing smaller, more useful molecules. When we specifically talk about alkanes breaking down into smaller alkanes and alkenes without air, we often use these terms interchangeably. This process is essential in the oil industry to convert heavy, less useful oil fractions into high-demand fuels like petrol.

questions.text: An isomer of ethanol is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This compound has the molecular formula C2H6O. Since ethanol also has the molecular formula C2H6O (C2H5OH), they are isomers. Specifically, they are functional isomers because they have the same atoms but different functional groups (ether vs alcohol).

The formula for diethyl ether is C4H10O. It contains four carbon atoms, which is double the number of carbons found in ethanol (C2). Because the molecular formulas are different, they cannot be isomers of each other.

Ethylene glycol has the formula C2H4(OH)2, which simplifies to C2H6O2. While it has the same number of carbons as ethanol, it has two oxygen atoms instead of one, so it does not share the same molecular composition.

Methanol is the simplest alcohol with the formula CH3OH (or CH4O). It only contains one carbon atom, whereas ethanol contains two. Since they have a different number of atoms, they are homologues, not isomers.

questions.explanation: Isomers must have the same number of every type of atom. Ethanol and dimethyl ether both contain 2 carbons, 6 hydrogens, and 1 oxygen atom, giving them both the formula C2H6O. In ethanol, the oxygen is part of an -OH group, but in dimethyl ether, the oxygen is linked between two methyl groups. This makes them functional group isomers.

questions.text: An atom or group of atoms which confers characteristic properties to an organic molecule is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A radical is a molecule with an unpaired electron, not a specific atom or group of atoms that confers characteristic properties to an organic molecule.
Correct! A functional group is an atom or group of atoms that imparts characteristic chemical properties to an organic molecule.
A polymer is a large molecule composed of repeating structural units, not a specific atom or group of atoms that confers characteristic properties to an organic molecule.
This option is incorrect as the correct answer is 'Functional group' which is the atom or group of atoms conferring characteristic properties to an organic molecule.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Functional group'. In organic chemistry, a functional group is a specific atom or group of atoms within a molecule that is responsible for the characteristic chemical reactions of that molecule. The other options are incorrect because they do not specifically refer to the atom or group of atoms conferring characteristic properties to an organic molecule.

questions.text: Compounds having same molecular formula but different structures are said to be
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Polymers are large molecules made up of repeating subunits. This term does not apply to compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures.

Isomers are compounds that have the same molecular formula but different structural formulas. This is the correct term for compounds with different structures despite having the same molecular formula.
Radicals are reactive species with unpaired electrons. This term does not relate to compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures.

Functional groups are specific atoms or groups of atoms within a molecule that are responsible for the characteristic chemical reactions of that molecule. This term does not describe compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures.

questions.explanation: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures are called isomers. Isomers exhibit different physical and chemical properties due to their distinct structural arrangements. Polymers, radicals, and functional groups do not specifically refer to compounds with identical molecular formulas and varying structures.

questions.text: The quality of petroleum is determined by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The decane number is not used to determine the quality of petroleum; it is the octane number that plays a significant role in this assessment.
Correct! The quality of petroleum is determined by its octane number, which measures its performance in internal combustion engines.
The hexane number is not used to determine the quality of petroleum; it is the octane number that is crucial in this evaluation.
This option is incorrect as the octane number is specifically used to assess the quality of petroleum.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Octane number. The quality of petroleum is determined by its octane number, which indicates how well the fuel performs in internal combustion engines. Decane and hexane numbers are not relevant in this context, making Options A and C incorrect. Option D is also incorrect, as the octane number is a key factor in assessing petroleum quality.

questions.text: _ of the following obeys isomerism
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon dioxide (CO2) does not have isomers as it is a simple linear molecule with no other possible structural arrangements.
Ethanol (C2H6O) has isomers due to the presence of a carbon chain and an alcohol functional group, allowing for different structural arrangements.
Copper sulfate (CuSO4) is a compound and does not exhibit isomerism as it has a fixed chemical composition.
This option is incorrect as C2H6O (Ethanol) does have isomers, making it the correct answer in this context.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: C2H6O. Ethanol has isomers due to the presence of a carbon chain and an alcohol functional group, allowing for different structural arrangements. Option A (CO2) is incorrect as it is a simple linear molecule without isomers. Option C (CuSO4) is also incorrect as it is a compound with a fixed chemical composition, not exhibiting isomerism. Option D (None of these) is incorrect because C2H6O (Ethanol) does have isomers, making it the correct answer.

questions.text: Two or more than two different compounds having the same molecular formula but different carbon chains or skeletons are said to be
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chain isomers refer to compounds with the same molecular formula but different carbon chains. In this case, the compounds have different arrangements of the carbon skeleton, leading to distinct properties and reactivities.
Position isomers have the same carbon skeleton, but the functional groups are attached at different positions on the chain. This differs from the scenario described in the question.
Functional group isomers have the same molecular formula and carbon chain, but different functional groups. This is not the case in the question.
Metamers are a type of chain isomer where the functional groups are attached to different carbon atoms in the chain. This is a specific subset of chain isomerism.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Chain isomers.' In this scenario, compounds with different carbon chains but the same molecular formula are known as chain isomers. Position isomers, functional group isomers, and metamers involve variations in different structural aspects, making them distinct from the situation described in the question.

questions.text: The kind of isomerism which depends upon the relative position of the group or the position of a double or single bond in case of unsaturated compounds is termed as
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This occurs when the arrangement of the carbon skeleton or "backbone" changes, such as moving from a straight chain to a branched chain. The position of functional groups doesn't necessarily change, but the actual length of the parent carbon chain does.

This type of isomerism specifically involves changing the location of a functional group, a substituent, or a multiple bond (double or triple bond) along the same carbon chain. The "home address" of the group changes, but the carbon skeleton remains identical.
Functional group isomerism involves compounds with different functional groups. This is not the correct type of isomerism described in the question.
Metamerism is a type of chain isomerism where different alkyl groups are attached to the same functional group. This is not the correct type of isomerism described in the question.

questions.explanation: Position isomerism is like keeping the same building (the carbon chain) but moving a window (the functional group) from the first floor to the second floor. In unsaturated compounds like alkenes or alkynes, the double or triple bond acts as the "group." If the bond moves from the first carbon to the second carbon in a four-carbon chain, you get 1-butene and 2-butene, which are classic position isomers.

questions.text: Isomerism which involves compounds having the same molecular formula but different functional groups are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chain isomerism refers to isomers that differ in the arrangement of carbon atoms in the main carbon chain.
Position isomerism occurs when functional groups are attached at different positions on the main carbon chain.
Functional group isomerism involves compounds with the same molecular formula but different functional groups.
Metamerism is a type of isomerism where compounds have the same alkyl groups attached to different functional groups.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Functional group isomerism.' This type of isomerism occurs when compounds have the same molecular formula but different functional groups. Chain isomerism and position isomerism involve differences in the carbon chain arrangement and functional group positioning, respectively. Metamerism involves the same alkyl groups attached to different functional groups, not different functional groups altogether.

questions.text: _Is exhibited by compounds having the same functional group but different alkyl groups attached to the same multivalent atom
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chain isomerism refers to compounds that have different arrangements of the carbon chain. This is not the correct term for compounds with the same functional group but different alkyl groups.

Position isomerism refers to compounds with the same molecular formula but different positioning of functional groups or substituents. This is not the correct term for compounds with different alkyl attachments.
Functional group isomerism refers to compounds with the same molecular formula but different functional groups. This is not the correct term for compounds with different alkyl attachments.

Metamerism is exhibited by compounds having the same functional group but different alkyl groups attached to the same multivalent atom. This term specifically describes the scenario mentioned in the question.

questions.explanation: Metamerism is the correct answer because it precisely matches the definition provided in the question. Chain isomerism, position isomerism, and functional group isomerism do not accurately describe compounds with the same functional group but different alkyl attachments. It is important to understand the specific definitions of isomerism types to correctly identify the relationship between compounds.

questions.text: In cracking usually catalyst usually used is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Platinum is a very expensive noble metal catalyst. While it is used in "catalytic reforming" to improve the octane number of gasoline, it is not the standard catalyst for the "cracking" process because it would be easily poisoned by impurities in the crude oil.

This is a mixture of silica SiO2) and alumina (Al2O3), often found in the form of zeolites. These are the most common catalysts for cracking because they have a porous structure and "acid sites" that help break large hydrocarbon bonds at lower temperatures.

Nickel is primarily used as a catalyst for "hydrogenation" reactions (adding hydrogen to unsaturated fats or oils). In the petroleum industry, it is also used in steam reforming to make hydrogen, but it is not the main catalyst used for breaking down long alkanes.

This is not a catalyst for cracking. It was historically used as an "anti-knock" additive in gasoline to make it burn more smoothly in car engines. It has since been phased out in most countries because of the environmental and health risks of lead pollution.

questions.explanation: In the industrial process called catalytic cracking, heavy oil is converted into lighter gasoline. Instead of using extremely high heat alone, we use a catalyst like aluminosilicate (zeolite). This catalyst allows the reaction to happen at a lower temperature (around 500°C) and produces more branched-chain alkanes, which make better fuel. The aluminosilicate acts as a solid acid that "snaps" the long carbon chains into smaller, more valuable pieces.

questions.text: Iso-butane exhibited
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chain isomerism occurs when isomers have different arrangements of carbon chains. In the case of iso-butane, the arrangement of the carbon chain differs from that of normal butane, leading to chain isomerism. This is the correct option.

Position isomerism involves isomers that differ in the position of functional groups or substituents. However, in the case of iso-butane, the difference lies in the arrangement of the carbon chain, not the position of functional groups. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

Functional group isomerism occurs when isomers have different functional groups. In the case of iso-butane, the functional groups remain the same (alkane), but the structure differs due to the branching of the carbon chain. Thus, this option is incorrect.

Metamerism is a type of structural isomerism where there is a difference in the alkyl groups on either side of the functional group. Since iso-butane does not exhibit this specific type of isomerism, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Iso-butane exhibits chain isomerism because it is an isomer of butane with a different arrangement of carbon atoms in the chain. This difference in structure leads to different physical and chemical properties compared to normal butane. The other options (position isomerism, functional group isomerism, metamerism) do not accurately describe the type of isomerism shown by iso-butane.

questions.text: In CCL4 molecule the four valencies of carbon atom are directed towards the corners of a
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In CCL4, the carbon atom is not arranged in a cube structure.
A hexagon is a flat, 2D shape and does not represent the 3D arrangement of atoms in CCL4.
A prism is a polyhedron with two parallel polygonal bases connected by lateral faces, not the arrangement seen in CCL4.
In a CCL4 molecule, the four valencies of the carbon atom are directed towards the corners of a tetrahedron, giving it a symmetrical 3D shape.

questions.explanation: In a CCL4 molecule, the carbon atom forms four single covalent bonds with the four chlorine atoms. This results in a tetrahedral geometry where the four valencies of the carbon atom are directed towards the corners of a tetrahedron. This arrangement helps in minimizing repulsions between the bonding electron pairs, leading to a stable molecular structure. The other options (cube, hexagon, prism) do not accurately represent the 3D arrangement of atoms in a CCL4 molecule.

questions.text: Compounds which contain a carbonyl group but differ from aldehydes in that two alkyl groups are attached to the carbon of the carbonyl group are called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ethers do not contain a carbonyl group, so they do not fit the description given in the question.
Correct! Ketones are compounds that contain a carbonyl group with two alkyl groups attached to the carbon of the carbonyl group.
Alcohols do not contain a carbonyl group, so they do not fit the description given in the question.
This option is incorrect as ketones specifically match the description provided in the question.

questions.text: As a number of carbon atoms in the homologous series of alkane molecules increases, for which property of the alkanes does the numerical value decrease
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As the number of carbon atoms increases, the molecular mass of the alkanes increases significantly. This causes the molecules to pack more closely together in the liquid state, leading to a gradual increase in density as you move down the homologous series.

Longer carbon chains have more electrons and a larger surface area, which leads to stronger London dispersion forces (intermolecular forces). More energy is required to overcome these forces to turn the liquid into a gas, so the enthalpy of vaporization increases.

With only 1-3 carbons, there is only 1 possible structure. However, as the number of carbons increases, the mathematical ways to arrange them (branches) grow rapidly. For example, butane has 2 isomers, while decane has 75, so the number increases dramatically.

Vapour pressure is the tendency of molecules to escape from a liquid into the gas phase. Because larger alkanes have stronger intermolecular "sticky" forces holding them together, fewer molecules can escape at a given temperature, causing the vapour pressure to decrease.

questions.explanation: In a homologous series, bigger molecules mean stronger intermolecular forces. Imagine the molecules as magnets; small alkanes like methane are weak magnets and fly away easily (high vapour pressure), while long alkanes like octane are strong magnets that prefer to stay clumped together as a liquid. Because these larger molecules are harder to pull apart, their boiling point goes up, but their tendency to evaporate, their vapour pressure, goes down.
